results.possibleIdentity: R.C. Erickson (likely Robert C. Erickson, Sr.) was a technical person and engineer active in the late 1960s and 1970s, associated with M&T Chemicals (Metal & Thermit Corporation) at their facility in Union Beach, New Jersey. According to records from the organization Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), Erickson was a member of their Technical Service Program, offering expertise in the field of "Machine Tool Controls." M&T Chemicals maintained a significant research and development laboratory and manufacturing plant at Front Street and Broadway in Union Beach during this period. The company was a major developer of organometallic chemistry and automated electroplating systems, which required complex machine controls for process automation. Robert C. Erickson, Sr. (1929–2013) was raised in Union Beach and later worked as a stationary engineer and technical supervisor, eventually retiring from Mars International (an engineering and manufacturing firm) in 2007. His affiliation with E.A.T. indicates he was among the engineers who volunteered to collaborate with artists to integrate technology into creative projects during the height of the organization's activity between 1966 and the early 1970s.
